scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The provided skill appears largely benign and implements a local feature-flag evaluation example without evident prompt injection, data exfiltration, command injection, obfuscation, or unsafe tool chaining. The main issues are low-severity packaging and metadata hygiene concerns: a missing referenced file and incomplete optional manifest fields.

meta_correlations.relationship: All findings relate to incomplete packaging metadata or documentation consistency rather than exploitable security behavior.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add a proper YAML manifest with license, compatibility, and allowed-tools fields, and either include the referenced file or remove/update the stale reference.

meta_recommendations.title: Add an explicit manifest block
meta_recommendations.fix:
Add frontmatter to SKILL.md such as: `---
name: feature-flags
description: Manage feature flags for gradual rollouts
license: MIT
compatibility: [Claude.ai, Claude Code]
allowed-tools: [Read]
---`

meta_recommendations.title: Resolve the missing file reference
meta_recommendations.fix: If `5.py` is required, add it to the package; otherwise remove the reference from the skill metadata/instructions so the package contents match the documentation.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill content appears benign and implements a local feature-flag evaluator; the reported issues are packaging/metadata hygiene items, not security threats.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Add complete manifest metadata and ensure any referenced files are actually included or remove stale references.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, credential access, or other harmful behavior is present in the provided SKILL.md code. Findings are informational and do not indicate malicious or unsafe functionality.
